context.pr_title: fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.
context.pr_body:
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README [//]: # (
) ([简体中文](./README_zh.md)|English) [//]: # (# FunASR: A Fundamental End-to-End Speech Recognition Toolkit) [![SVG Banners](https://svg-banners.vercel.app/api?type=origin&text1=FunASR🤠&text2=💖%20A%20Fundamental%20End-to-End%20Speech%20Recognition%20Toolkit&width=800&height=210)](https://github.com/Akshay090/svg-banners) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/funasr)](https://pypi.org/project/funasr/)

FunASR hopes to build a bridge between academic research and industrial applications on speech recognition. By supporting the training & finetuning of the industrial-grade speech recognition model, researchers and developers can conduct research and production of speech recognition models more conveniently, and promote the development of speech recognition ecology. ASR for Fun！ [**Highlights**](#highlights) | [**News**](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR#whats-new) | [**Installation**](#installation) | [**Quick Start**](#quick-start) | [**Tutorial**](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR/blob/main/docs/tutorial/README.md) | [**Runtime**](./runtime/readme.md) | [**Model Zoo**](#model-zoo) | [**Contact**](#contact) ## Highlights - FunASR is a fundamental speech recognition toolkit that offers a variety of features, including speech recognition (ASR), Voice Activity Detection (VAD), Punctuation Restoration, Language Models, Speaker Verification, Speaker Diarization and multi-talker ASR. FunASR provides convenient scripts and tutorials, supporting inference and fine-tuning of pre-trained models. - We have released a vast collection of academic and industrial pretrained models on the [ModelScope](https://www.modelscope.cn/models?page=1&tasks=auto-speech-recognition) and [huggingface](https://huggingface.co/FunASR), which can be accessed through our [Model Zoo](https://github.com/alibaba-damo-academy/FunASR/blob/main/docs/model_zoo/modelscope_models.md). The representative [Paraformer-large](https://www.modelscope.cn/models/damo/speech_paraformer-large_asr_nat-zh-cn-16k-common-vocab8404-pytorch/summary), a non-autoregressive end-to-end speech recognition model, has the advantages of high accuracy, high efficiency, and convenient deployment, supporting the rapid construction of speech recognition services. For more details on service deployment, please refer to the [service deployment document](runtime/readme_cn.md). ## What's new: - 2024/10/29: Real-time Transcription Service 1.12 released, The 2pass-offline mode supports the SensevoiceSmal model；([docs](runtime/readme.md)); - 2024/10/10：Added support for the W ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.

## Description
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

arguments.reason: Check whether the same argmax/unique logic exists in the ONNXRuntime implementation and should be updated for consistency.
arguments.expected_information: Any use of argmax with dim parameter or np.unique that differs from torch.unique_consecutive behavior.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py` implements an ONNXRuntime-based inference wrapper (`SenseVoiceSmall`) for SenseVoice ASR, including audio loading, feature extraction, ONNX model execution, and token decoding.

### Architecture
- **Initialization** (`SenseVoiceSmall.__init__`) resolves a model directory (local or downloaded via ModelScope), ensures an ONNX model exists (exporting via `funasr.AutoModel` if needed), loads configuration and CMVN, then constructs:
  - `SentencepiecesTokenizer` for token-to-text decoding
  - `WavFrontend` for fbank + LFR/CMVN feature extraction
  - `OrtInferSession` for ONNXRuntime inference
- **Inference flow** (`SenseVoiceSmall.__call__`):
  1. Parse language/textnorm tags via `read_tags()`, `_get_lid()`, `_get_tnid()`
  2. Load waveforms via `load_data()`
  3. Extract padded features via `extract_feat()` + `pad_feats()`
  4. Run ONNX via `infer()`
  5. Decode CTC output with an **argmax + unique** post-processing step, then `SentencepiecesTokenizer.decode(...)`

### Functions & Classes
- `class SenseVoiceSmall` — Main user-facing class encapsulating model setup and end-to-end ASR inference (audio → text). Central to any decoding/post-processing consistency changes.
  - `__init__(self, model_dir: Union[str, Path] = None, batch_size: int = 1, device_id: Union[str, int] = "-1", plot_timestamp_to: str = "", quantize: bool = False, intra_op_num_threads: int = 4, cache_dir: str = None, **kwargs)` — Prepares model artifacts and runtime components:
    - Downloads model via `modelscope.hub.snapshot_download` if `model_dir` doesn’t exist.
    - Selects `model.onnx` vs `model_quant.onnx`; exports to ONNX via `funasr.AutoModel(...).export(...)` if missing.
    - Loads YAML via `read_yaml`, sets CMVN file, creates `WavFrontend`, `OrtInferSession`, and tokenizer.
    - Defines `self.blank_id = 0` and language/textnorm mappings used by `read_tags()`.
  - `_get_lid(self, lid)` — Maps a language string key (`"auto"`, `"zh"`, etc.) to an integer tag; raises `ValueError` for unknown keys.
  - `_get_tnid(self, tnid)` — Maps a text normalization mode string (`"withitn"`, `"woitn"`) to an integer tag; raises `ValueError` for unknown keys.
  - `read_tags(self, language_input, textnorm_input)` — Parses `language` and `textnorm` inputs from:
    - list of strings, a single string, or a path to a file containing one tag per line
    - Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)` of integer IDs used as ONNX inputs.
  - `__call__(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], **kwargs)` — Executes batched ASR inference and decoding.
    - Loads audio via `load_data()`, extracts features via `extract_feat()`, runs `infer()`.
    - **CTC decoding logic (key for your goal):**
      - `x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]`
      - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`  *(uses a PyTorch-style signature, but `x` is a NumPy slice; see note below)*
      - `yseq = np.unique(yseq)`  *(global unique, sorted unique values; not consecutive-collapse)*
      - removes blanks: `mask = yseq != self.blank_id`
      - decodes: `self.tokenizer.decode(token_int)`
    - Returns `asr_res` list of decoded strings.
  - `load_data(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], fs: int = None) -> List` — Loads audio into numpy waveforms.
    - Accepts a numpy waveform, a path, or list of paths.
    - Includes nested helpers:
      - `convert_to_wav(input_path, output_path)` — Converts MP3 to WAV via `pydub.AudioSegment`.
      - `load_wav(path: str) -> np.ndarray` — Loads WAV via `librosa.load`, optionally converting non-wav to wav first.
  - `extract_feat(self, waveform_list: List[np.ndarray]) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs frontend feature extraction per waveform:
    - `self.frontend.fbank(waveform)` then `self.frontend.lfr_cmvn(speech)`
    - Pads to max length via `pad_feats()`
    - Returns `(feats, feats_len)` as `np.float32` and `np.int32`.
  - `pad_feats(feats: List[np.ndarray], max_feat_len: int) -> np.ndarray` *(staticmethod)* — Zero-pads each `[T, D]` feature matrix to `[max_feat_len, D]`, stacks into `[B, T, D]`.
  - `infer(self, feats: np.ndarray, feats_len: np.ndarray, language: np.ndarray, textnorm: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs ONNXRuntime inference via `self.ort_infer([feats, feats_len, language, textnorm])` and returns model outputs.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- This file **does contain** the argmax/unique logic you’re looking for, and it differs from `torch.unique_consecutive`-style CTC collapse:
  - `yseq = np.unique(yseq)` removes duplicates **globally** and also sorts, which can destroy token order and does **not** match `torch.unique_consecutive` (which only removes adjacent repeats and preserves order).
- It also calls `x.argmax(dim=-1)`, which is a PyTorch API; with NumPy arrays this should be `x.argmax(axis=-1)`. If this code currently runs, `ctc_logits` might be something other than a pure NumPy array at runtime, but as written it is inconsistent with typical ONNXRuntime NumPy outputs.
- If the PR goal is consistency with torch decoding (argmax over last dim + consecutive dedup + blank removal), the key update point is inside `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` where `np.unique` is applied.

### File Overview
`runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py` implements an ONNXRuntime-based inference wrapper (`SenseVoiceSmall`) for SenseVoice ASR, including audio loading, feature extraction, ONNX model execution, and token decoding.

### Architecture
- **Initialization** (`SenseVoiceSmall.__init__`) resolves a model directory (local or downloaded via ModelScope), ensures an ONNX model exists (exporting via `funasr.AutoModel` if needed), loads configuration and CMVN, then constructs:
  - `SentencepiecesTokenizer` for token-to-text decoding
  - `WavFrontend` for fbank + LFR/CMVN feature extraction
  - `OrtInferSession` for ONNXRuntime inference
- **Inference flow** (`SenseVoiceSmall.__call__`):
  1. Parse language/textnorm tags via `read_tags()`, `_get_lid()`, `_get_tnid()`
  2. Load waveforms via `load_data()`
  3. Extract padded features via `extract_feat()` + `pad_feats()`
  4. Run ONNX via `infer()`
  5. Decode CTC output with an **argmax + unique** post-processing step, then `SentencepiecesTokenizer.decode(...)`

### Functions & Classes
- `class SenseVoiceSmall` — Main user-facing class encapsulating model setup and end-to-end ASR inference (audio → text). Central to any decoding/post-processing consistency changes.
  - `__init__(self, model_dir: Union[str, Path] = None, batch_size: int = 1, device_id: Union[str, int] = "-1", plot_timestamp_to: str = "", quantize: bool = False, intra_op_num_threads: int = 4, cache_dir: str = None, **kwargs)` — Prepares model artifacts and runtime components:
    - Downloads model via `modelscope.hub.snapshot_download` if `model_dir` doesn’t exist.
    - Selects `model.onnx` vs `model_quant.onnx`; exports to ONNX via `funasr.AutoModel(...).export(...)` if missing.
    - Loads YAML via `read_yaml`, sets CMVN file, creates `WavFrontend`, `OrtInferSession`, and tokenizer.
    - Defines `self.blank_id = 0` and language/textnorm mappings used by `read_tags()`.
  - `_get_lid(self, lid)` — Maps a language string key (`"auto"`, `"zh"`, etc.) to an integer tag; raises `ValueError` for unknown keys.
  - `_get_tnid(self, tnid)` — Maps a text normalization mode string (`"withitn"`, `"woitn"`) to an integer tag; raises `ValueError` for unknown keys.
  - `read_tags(self, language_input, textnorm_input)` — Parses `language` and `textnorm` inputs from:
    - list of strings, a single string, or a path to a file containing one tag per line
    - Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)` of integer IDs used as ONNX inputs.
  - `__call__(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], **kwargs)` — Executes batched ASR inference and decoding.
    - Loads audio via `load_data()`, extracts features via `extract_feat()`, runs `infer()`.
    - **CTC decoding logic (key for your goal):**
      - `x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]`
      - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`  *(uses a PyTorch-style signature, but `x` is a NumPy slice; see note below)*
      - `yseq = np.unique(yseq)`  *(global unique, sorted unique values; not consecutive-collapse)*
      - removes blanks: `mask = yseq != self.blank_id`
      - decodes: `self.tokenizer.decode(token_int)`
    - Returns `asr_res` list of decoded strings.
  - `load_data(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], fs: int = None) -> List` — Loads audio into numpy waveforms.
    - Accepts a numpy waveform, a path, or list of paths.
    - Includes nested helpers:
      - `convert_to_wav(input_path, output_path)` — Converts MP3 to WAV via `pydub.AudioSegment`.
      - `load_wav(path: str) -> np.ndarray` — Loads WAV via `librosa.load`, optionally converting non-wav to wav first.
  - `extract_feat(self, waveform_list: List[np.ndarray]) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs frontend feature extraction per waveform:
    - `self.frontend.fbank(waveform)` then `self.frontend.lfr_cmvn(speech)`
    - Pads to max length via `pad_feats()`
    - Returns `(feats, feats_len)` as `np.float32` and `np.int32`.
  - `pad_feats(feats: List[np.ndarray], max_feat_len: int) -> np.ndarray` *(staticmethod)* — Zero-pads each `[T, D]` feature matrix to `[max_feat_len, D]`, stacks into `[B, T, D]`.
  - `infer(self, feats: np.ndarray, feats_len: np.ndarray, language: np.ndarray, textnorm: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs ONNXRuntime inference via `self.ort_infer([feats, feats_len, language, textnorm])` and returns model outputs.

  - `yseq = np.unique(yseq)` removes duplicates **globally** and also sorts, which can destroy token order and does **not** match `torch.unique_consecutive` (which only removes adjacent repeats and preserves order).

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py` defines a lightweight TorchScript-based inference wrapper (`SenseVoiceSmall`) for SenseVoice ASR models, handling model loading/export, audio feature extraction, batched inference, and decoding.

### Architecture
- **Initialization path (`__init__`)**: resolves `model_dir` (local or ModelScope download), ensures a TorchScript model exists (optionally exporting via `funasr.AutoModel`), loads YAML config/CMVN, constructs the frontend (`WavFrontend`), loads the TorchScript model with `torch.jit.load`, and initializes tokenization and tag mappings.
- **Inference path (`__call__`)**:
  1. Parses language/text normalization tags via `read_tags`.
  2. Loads waveforms via `load_data`.
  3. Extracts features via `extract_feat` (which uses `pad_feats`).
  4. Runs TorchScript model (`self.ort_infer`) to get `ctc_logits` and `encoder_out_lens`.
  5. Decodes per sample by computing `yseq` from `ctc_logits` using `argmax`, then deduplicates using `torch.unique_consecutive`, removes blanks, and detokenizes via `self.tokenizer.decode`.

### Functions & Classes
- `class SenseVoiceSmall` — Main inference wrapper class; encapsulates model loading, frontend feature extraction, batched TorchScript inference, and CTC-style decoding. **This is where the argmax + unique logic lives.**

- `SenseVoiceSmall.__init__` — Sets up the runtime:
  - Downloads model snapshots if needed, selects `model.torchscript` vs `model_quant.torchscript`, exports TorchScript if missing.
  - Loads config (`read_yaml`), sets CMVN, constructs `SentencepiecesTokenizer` and `WavFrontend`, loads TorchScript model (`torch.jit.load`).
  - Initializes `self.blank_id` and tag dictionaries (`self.lid_dict`, `self.textnorm_dict`, plus int maps).
  - Important because it defines decoding assumptions like `self.blank_id = 0` used later in blank filtering.

- `SenseVoiceSmall._get_lid` — Maps a language string (e.g., `"en"`, `"auto"`) to an integer ID using `self.lid_dict`. Raises on unsupported language. (Note: the error message references `l` which is undefined; not the stated goal, but it is a bug.)
  
- `SenseVoiceSmall._get_tnid` — Maps a text normalization string (`"withitn"`, `"woitn"`) to an integer ID using `self.textnorm_dict`. Raises on unsupported input.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.read_tags` — Parses `language_input` and `textnorm_input`, supporting:
  - direct string tag,
  - list of tags,
  - path to a file containing tags line-by-line.
  Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)` aligned for batching. This impacts the tensors passed into `self.ort_infer`.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` — Primary inference entrypoint; **this contains the exact argmax and unique usage the agent is looking for**:
  - Batches audio, extracts features, expands `_language_list` and `_textnorm_list` to match batch size.
  - Runs model: `ctc_logits, encoder_out_lens = self.ort_infer(...)`.
  - Per batch element decoding (key lines):
    - `x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]`
    - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`
    - `yseq = torch.unique_consecutive(yseq, dim=-1)`
    - `mask = yseq != self.blank_id`
    - `token_int = yseq[mask].tolist()`
    - `asr_res.append(self.tokenizer.decode(token_int))`
  This is the exact surrounding logic where any incorrect `argmax` dimension choice or deduplication behavior (`torch.unique_consecutive`) would need to be changed.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.load_data` — Loads audio into numpy waveforms:
  - Accepts `np.ndarray`, a single filepath string, or a list of filepaths.
  - Uses `librosa.load(path, sr=fs)` to resample to frontend sample rate.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.extract_feat` — Converts waveforms to model features:
  - Uses `self.frontend.fbank(waveform)` then `self.frontend.lfr_cmvn(speech)`.
  - Pads to max length via `self.pad_feats`.
  - Returns `(feats, feats_len)` as numpy arrays.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.pad_feats` (static) — Pads a list of 2D feature matrices to `max_feat_len` along time dimension with zeros and returns a float32 numpy array.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The incorrect `argmax` and `unique` usage would be in `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__`, specifically the decoding block:
  - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`
  - `yseq = torch.unique_consecutive(yseq, dim=-1)`
  - followed by blank filtering and `self.tokenizer.decode`.
- If the PR/issue claims `argmax` is applied over the wrong axis or deduplication is done incorrectly (e.g., should operate per-time step, should not pass `dim=-1`, should handle batching differently, or should use a different collapsing strategy), **`SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` is the exact function to modify**, and the `x = ctc_logits[...]` slicing immediately above it provides the necessary context (shape/time truncation via `encoder_out_lens`).

### File Overview
`runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py` defines a lightweight TorchScript-based inference wrapper (`SenseVoiceSmall`) for SenseVoice ASR models, handling model loading/export, audio feature extraction, batched inference, and decoding.

### Architecture
- **Initialization path (`__init__`)**: resolves `model_dir` (local or ModelScope download), ensures a TorchScript model exists (optionally exporting via `funasr.AutoModel`), loads YAML config/CMVN, constructs the frontend (`WavFrontend`), loads the TorchScript model with `torch.jit.load`, and initializes tokenization and tag mappings.
- **Inference path (`__call__`)**:
  1. Parses language/text normalization tags via `read_tags`.
  2. Loads waveforms via `load_data`.
  3. Extracts features via `extract_feat` (which uses `pad_feats`).
  4. Runs TorchScript model (`self.ort_infer`) to get `ctc_logits` and `encoder_out_lens`.
  5. Decodes per sample by computing `yseq` from `ctc_logits` using `argmax`, then deduplicates using `torch.unique_consecutive`, removes blanks, and detokenizes via `self.tokenizer.decode`.

### Functions & Classes
- `class SenseVoiceSmall` — Main inference wrapper class; encapsulates model loading, frontend feature extraction, batched TorchScript inference, and CTC-style decoding. **This is where the argmax + unique logic lives.**

- `SenseVoiceSmall.__init__` — Sets up the runtime:
  - Downloads model snapshots if needed, selects `model.torchscript` vs `model_quant.torchscript`, exports TorchScript if missing.
  - Loads config (`read_yaml`), sets CMVN, constructs `SentencepiecesTokenizer` and `WavFrontend`, loads TorchScript model (`torch.jit.load`).
  - Initializes `self.blank_id` and tag dictionaries (`self.lid_dict`, `self.textnorm_dict`, plus int maps).
  - Important because it defines decoding assumptions like `self.blank_id = 0` used later in blank filtering.

- `SenseVoiceSmall._get_lid` — Maps a language string (e.g., `"en"`, `"auto"`) to an integer ID using `self.lid_dict`. Raises on unsupported language. (Note: the error message references `l` which is undefined; not the stated goal, but it is a bug.)
  
- `SenseVoiceSmall._get_tnid` — Maps a text normalization string (`"withitn"`, `"woitn"`) to an integer ID using `self.textnorm_dict`. Raises on unsupported input.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.read_tags` — Parses `language_input` and `textnorm_input`, supporting:
  - direct string tag,
  - list of tags,
  - path to a file containing tags line-by-line.
  Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)` aligned for batching. This impacts the tensors passed into `self.ort_infer`.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` — Primary inference entrypoint; **this contains the exact argmax and unique usage the agent is looking for**:
  - Batches audio, extracts features, expands `_language_list` and `_textnorm_list` to match batch size.
  - Runs model: `ctc_logits, encoder_out_lens = self.ort_infer(...)`.
  - Per batch element decoding (key lines):
    - `x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]`
    - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`
    - `yseq = torch.unique_consecutive(yseq, dim=-1)`
    - `mask = yseq != self.blank_id`
    - `token_int = yseq[mask].tolist()`
    - `asr_res.append(self.tokenizer.decode(token_int))`
  This is the exact surrounding logic where any incorrect `argmax` dimension choice or deduplication behavior (`torch.unique_consecutive`) would need to be changed.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.load_data` — Loads audio into numpy waveforms:
  - Accepts `np.ndarray`, a single filepath string, or a list of filepaths.
  - Uses `librosa.load(path, sr=fs)` to resample to frontend sample rate.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.extract_feat` — Converts waveforms to model features:
  - Uses `self.frontend.fbank(waveform)` then `self.frontend.lfr_cmvn(speech)`.
  - Pads to max length via `self.pad_feats`.
  - Returns `(feats, feats_len)` as numpy arrays.

- `SenseVoiceSmall.pad_feats` (static) — Pads a list of 2D feature matrices to `max_feat_len` along time dimension with zeros and returns a float32 numpy array.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The incorrect `argmax` and `unique` usage would be in `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__`, specifically the decoding block:
  - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)`
  - `yseq = torch.unique_consecutive(yseq, dim=-1)`
  - followed by blank filtering and `self.tokenizer.decode`.
- If the PR/issue claims `argmax` is applied over the wrong axis or deduplication is done incorrectly (e.g., should operate per-time step, should not pass `dim=-1`, should handle batching differently, or should use a different collapsing strategy), **`SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` is the exact function to modify**, and the `x = ctc_logits[...]` slicing immediately above it provides the necessary context (shape/time truncation via `encoder_out_lens`).

arguments.reason: Extract exact code lines around argmax/unique in __call__ for precise modification targets.
arguments.expected_information: Exact implementation of decoding block in SenseVoiceSmall.__call__ and any helper functions used.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
`runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py` defines a lightweight ONNX Runtime wrapper class `SenseVoiceSmall` that loads/export-downloads a SenseVoice model, extracts audio features, runs ONNX inference, and performs a very simple CTC-style greedy decode.

### Architecture
- **Model/bootstrap** in `SenseVoiceSmall.__init__`: resolves `model_dir` (local or ModelScope download), optionally exports to ONNX, loads YAML config/CMVN, then wires up:
  - `SentencepiecesTokenizer` for token-id → text decoding
  - `WavFrontend` for fbank + LFR/CMVN feature extraction
  - `OrtInferSession` for ONNX inference
- **Request path** in `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__`:
  1. Parse `language` and `textnorm` tags via `read_tags()`.
  2. Load waveforms via `load_data()`.
  3. Batch: `extract_feat()` → `infer()` → per-sample decode (argmax + unique + blank removal) → `tokenizer.decode()`.

### Functions & Classes
- `SenseVoiceSmall` — Main inference/decoding wrapper around the ONNX model, frontend feature extraction, and tokenizer decoding. Central class for modifications to decoding behavior.
  - `__init__(self, model_dir: Union[str, Path] = None, batch_size: int = 1, device_id: Union[str, int] = "-1", plot_timestamp_to: str = "", quantize: bool = False, intra_op_num_threads: int = 4, cache_dir: str = None, **kwargs)` — Initializes model assets and runtime components:
    - Downloads model via `snapshot_download` if `model_dir` doesn’t exist.
    - Exports ONNX via `funasr.AutoModel(...).export(...)` if `.onnx` missing.
    - Loads `config.yaml`, sets CMVN path, creates `SentencepiecesTokenizer`, `WavFrontend`, and `OrtInferSession`.
    - Defines `self.blank_id = 0` plus language/textnorm tag mappings (`self.lid_dict`, `self.lid_int_dict`, `self.textnorm_dict`, `self.textnorm_int_dict`).
  - `_get_lid(self, lid)` — Maps a language string key (e.g. `"auto"`, `"zh"`) to its integer tag id using `self.lid_dict`. Raises `ValueError` if unsupported.
  - `_get_tnid(self, tnid)` — Maps a text normalization string key (`"withitn"`, `"woitn"`) to integer tag id using `self.textnorm_dict`. Raises `ValueError` if unsupported.
  - `read_tags(self, language_input, textnorm_input)` — Normalizes `language` and `textnorm` inputs into integer lists. Accepts:
    - `list` of strings
    - `str` either as a direct tag value or as a file path containing one tag per line
    Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)`.
  - `__call__(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], **kwargs)` — End-to-end inference entry point. Key responsibilities:
    - Reads `language`/`textnorm` from kwargs (defaults: `"auto"`, `"woitn"`) and converts to lists via `read_tags()`.
    - Loads waveforms via `load_data()`, extracts features via `extract_feat()`, runs inference via `infer()`.
    - **Decoding block (the exact argmax/unique section the agent is targeting):**
      ```python
            for b in range(feats.shape[0]):
                # back to torch.Tensor
                # if isinstance(ctc_logits, np.ndarray):
                #     ctc_logits = torch.from_numpy(ctc_logits).float()
                # support batch_size=1 only currently
                x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]
                yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)
                yseq = np.unique(yseq)

                mask = yseq != self.blank_id
                token_int = yseq[mask].tolist()

                asr_res.append(self.tokenizer.decode(token_int))
      ```
      - `x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]` slices valid time steps.
      - `yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1)` performs greedy per-frame token selection (**note**: uses `dim` like PyTorch; if `x` is a NumPy array, this is inconsistent with NumPy’s `axis` API).
      - `yseq = np.unique(yseq)` collapses tokens by taking *set-unique values*, not standard CTC “collapse repeats while preserving order”.
      - Removes blanks (`self.blank_id`) and decodes remaining token ids with `self.tokenizer.decode(token_int)`.
  - `load_data(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], fs: int = None) -> List` — Loads input audio into a list of 1D float waveforms.
    - Accepts `np.ndarray` (already waveform), `str` (path), or `list[str]` (paths).
    - Internally defines:
      - `convert_to_wav(input_path, output_path)` — Uses `pydub.AudioSegment` to convert MP3 to WAV.
      - `load_wav(path: str) -> np.ndarray` — Converts non-`.wav` to `.wav` then loads via `librosa.load(path, sr=fs)`.
  - `extract_feat(self, waveform_list: List[np.ndarray]) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs frontend feature extraction:
    - `self.frontend.fbank(waveform)` then `self.frontend.lfr_cmvn(speech)`.
    - Validates non-empty speech, pads features via `pad_feats()`, returns `(feats, feats_len)` where `feats_len` is `np.int32`.
  - `pad_feats(feats: List[np.ndarray], max_feat_len: int) -> np.ndarray` *(static)* — Pads each `[T, D]` feature matrix to `max_feat_len` with zeros and stacks into a float32 batch array.
  - `infer(self, feats: np.ndarray, feats_len: np.ndarray, language: np.ndarray, textnorm: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]` — Runs ONNX inference via `self.ort_infer([feats, feats_len, language, textnorm])` and returns the raw model outputs (expected: `ctc_logits, encoder_out_lens`).

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
- The exact decoding logic in `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` is where `argmax` and `np.unique` are applied; those are the precise modification targets the agent wants.
- No helper function encapsulates decoding; changes must be made directly in `__call__` (or by introducing a new helper and calling it there).
- The current block mixes PyTorch-style `argmax(dim=...)` with NumPy’s `np.unique`, which is important context if the agent is fixing correctness or portability of decoding.

  - `_get_lid(self, lid)` — Maps a language string key (e.g. `"auto"`, `"zh"`) to its integer tag id using `self.lid_dict`. Raises `ValueError` if unsupported.

  - `read_tags(self, language_input, textnorm_input)` — Normalizes `language` and `textnorm` inputs into integer lists. Accepts:
    - `list` of strings
    - `str` either as a direct tag value or as a file path containing one tag per line
    Returns `(language_list, textnorm_list)`.
  - `__call__(self, wav_content: Union[str, np.ndarray, List[str]], **kwargs)` — End-to-end inference entry point. Key responsibilities:
    - Reads `language`/`textnorm` from kwargs (defaults: `"auto"`, `"woitn"`) and converts to lists via `read_tags()`.
    - Loads waveforms via `load_data()`, extracts features via `extract_feat()`, runs inference via `infer()`.
    - **Decoding block (the exact argmax/unique section the agent is targeting):**
      ```python
            for b in range(feats.shape[0]):
                # back to torch.Tensor
                # if isinstance(ctc_logits, np.ndarray):
                #     ctc_logits = torch.from_numpy(ctc_logits).float()
                # support batch_size=1 only currently
                x = ctc_logits[b, : encoder_out_lens[b].item(), :]

                yseq = np.unique(yseq)
      ```

output.pr_title: fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.
output.pr_body:
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。
output.masca_optimized:
### MASCA (PR-focused): Fix SenseVoice ONNX decoding (argmax + unique)

#### M — Mission
Align SenseVoice ONNXRuntime greedy CTC decoding behavior with the Torch implementation:
1) Replace the invalid NumPy call `x.argmax(dim=-1)` with `np.argmax(x, axis=-1)`.
2) Replace `np.unique(yseq)` (global unique + sorting) with “unique consecutive” behavior that only removes *adjacent* duplicates while preserving order, matching `torch.unique_consecutive` used in the TorchScript path.

#### A — Affected Area(s)
- ONNXRuntime inference wrapper for SenseVoice Small:
  - `runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py`
- (Reference behavior) TorchScript wrapper already does the correct consecutive-dedup:
  - `runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py`

#### S — Scope of Change
- Change only the decoding block inside `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` in the ONNX file:
  - Compute `yseq` via NumPy argmax on last axis.
  - Apply consecutive-dedup mask (`np.diff` + prepend True), then blank filtering, then decode.
- No model export / frontend / Ort session changes required.

#### C — Constraints / Correctness Requirements
- Must handle `yseq` as a 1-D NumPy array of token IDs.
- Must preserve token order and only collapse consecutive repeats (CTC greedy collapse).
- Must remain compatible with `encoder_out_lens[b].item()` slicing.
- Keep blank removal semantics (`self.blank_id`, currently 0).

#### A — Acceptance Criteria
- Running ONNX SenseVoice inference no longer throws `TypeError` due to `dim` argument.
- Output transcripts match the TorchScript path more closely (no loss of order due to sorting, and no removal of non-consecutive repeats).
- Unit/manual test: a synthetic sequence like `[1,1,2,1]` collapses to `[1,2,1]` (not `[1,2]` as `np.unique` would do).

#### Risks / Edge Cases
- If `yseq` can be empty, `np.diff(yseq)` yields empty; mask construction must still work (it does with `np.concatenate(([True], ...))` only when `yseq` is non-empty). If empty sequences are possible, guard with `if yseq.size > 0:`.
- Ensure `yseq` dtype supports `np.diff` (int types are fine).

files_to_modify.reason: Contains the buggy decoding block: uses `x.argmax(dim=-1)` (invalid for NumPy) and `np.unique(yseq)` (incorrect semantics vs `torch.unique_consecutive`). Must be updated to `np.argmax(..., axis=-1)` and consecutive-dedup masking.

functions_to_modify.reason: This function performs greedy decoding after ONNX inference. It currently calls `x.argmax(dim=-1)` and `np.unique(yseq)`. Replace with NumPy-correct argmax and implement unique-consecutive behavior (mask via `np.diff`).

output.analysis_summary: Searched the repository for SenseVoice implementations and found two relevant wrappers: a TorchScript version (`runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py`) and an ONNXRuntime version (`runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py`). The TorchScript path already uses `x.argmax(dim=-1)` on a Torch tensor and `torch.unique_consecutive`, which matches expected CTC greedy collapsing. The ONNXRuntime path slices `ctc_logits` (NumPy) and incorrectly uses the Torch-style `argmax(dim=...)` plus `np.unique`, which globally deduplicates and sorts, breaking token order and semantics. Therefore the only required code change is inside `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` in the ONNXRuntime file, updating argmax to `np.argmax(x, axis=-1)` and replacing `np.unique` with a consecutive-dedup mask implementation.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```
agents.input_prompt:
Generate an implementation plan for this PR:

## PR Title
fix #2331: solve problems in sensevoice_bin.py related to argmax and unique operations.

## PR Body
如issue #2331 提及，修复两个问题：
1. yseq = x.argmax(dim=-1) 改为 yseq = np.argmax(x, axis=-1)：

    - 由于 np.argmax 没有 dim 参数，原代码会导致 TypeError。改为 np.argmax(x, axis=-1) 以正确计算最大值的索引。    

3. 原来yseq = np.unique(yseq) 跟torch.unique_consecutive不一致（torch.unique_consecutive返回的结果是正确的），查了下：torch.unique_consecutive 只去除连续重复的元素，而np.unique：去除所有重复的元素，并返回排序后的唯一值数组。所以需要改为：

> mask = np.concatenate(([True], np.diff(yseq) != 0))
yseq = yseq[mask]

说明如下：
- np.diff(yseq) 计算数组中相邻元素的差值。
- np.diff(yseq) != 0 生成一个布尔数组，表示哪些位置的元素与前一个元素不同。
- np.concatenate(([True], ...)) 在布尔数组前添加一个 True，以保留第一个元素。
- yseq[mask] 使用布尔掩码提取去重后的数组。

经过测试后，结果符合预期。

## MASCA Analysis (optimized)
### MASCA (PR-focused): Fix SenseVoice ONNX decoding (argmax + unique)

#### M — Mission
Align SenseVoice ONNXRuntime greedy CTC decoding behavior with the Torch implementation:
1) Replace the invalid NumPy call `x.argmax(dim=-1)` with `np.argmax(x, axis=-1)`.
2) Replace `np.unique(yseq)` (global unique + sorting) with “unique consecutive” behavior that only removes *adjacent* duplicates while preserving order, matching `torch.unique_consecutive` used in the TorchScript path.

#### A — Affected Area(s)
- ONNXRuntime inference wrapper for SenseVoice Small:
  - `runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py`
- (Reference behavior) TorchScript wrapper already does the correct consecutive-dedup:
  - `runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py`

#### S — Scope of Change
- Change only the decoding block inside `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` in the ONNX file:
  - Compute `yseq` via NumPy argmax on last axis.
  - Apply consecutive-dedup mask (`np.diff` + prepend True), then blank filtering, then decode.
- No model export / frontend / Ort session changes required.

#### C — Constraints / Correctness Requirements
- Must handle `yseq` as a 1-D NumPy array of token IDs.
- Must preserve token order and only collapse consecutive repeats (CTC greedy collapse).
- Must remain compatible with `encoder_out_lens[b].item()` slicing.
- Keep blank removal semantics (`self.blank_id`, currently 0).

#### A — Acceptance Criteria
- Running ONNX SenseVoice inference no longer throws `TypeError` due to `dim` argument.
- Output transcripts match the TorchScript path more closely (no loss of order due to sorting, and no removal of non-consecutive repeats).
- Unit/manual test: a synthetic sequence like `[1,1,2,1]` collapses to `[1,2,1]` (not `[1,2]` as `np.unique` would do).

#### Risks / Edge Cases
- If `yseq` can be empty, `np.diff(yseq)` yields empty; mask construction must still work (it does with `np.concatenate(([True], ...))` only when `yseq` is non-empty). If empty sequences are possible, guard with `if yseq.size > 0:`.
- Ensure `yseq` dtype supports `np.diff` (int types are fine).

## Files to Modify
- runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py: Contains the buggy decoding block: uses `x.argmax(dim=-1)` (invalid for NumPy) and `np.unique(yseq)` (incorrect semantics vs `torch.unique_consecutive`). Must be updated to `np.argmax(..., axis=-1)` and consecutive-dedup masking.

## Functions to Modify
- SenseVoiceSmall.__call__ (runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py): This function performs greedy decoding after ONNX inference. It currently calls `x.argmax(dim=-1)` and `np.unique(yseq)`. Replace with NumPy-correct argmax and implement unique-consecutive behavior (mask via `np.diff`).

## Analysis Summary
Searched the repository for SenseVoice implementations and found two relevant wrappers: a TorchScript version (`runtime/python/libtorch/funasr_torch/sensevoice_bin.py`) and an ONNXRuntime version (`runtime/python/onnxruntime/funasr_onnx/sensevoice_bin.py`). The TorchScript path already uses `x.argmax(dim=-1)` on a Torch tensor and `torch.unique_consecutive`, which matches expected CTC greedy collapsing. The ONNXRuntime path slices `ctc_logits` (NumPy) and incorrectly uses the Torch-style `argmax(dim=...)` plus `np.unique`, which globally deduplicates and sorts, breaking token order and semantics. Therefore the only required code change is inside `SenseVoiceSmall.__call__` in the ONNXRuntime file, updating argmax to `np.argmax(x, axis=-1)` and replacing `np.unique` with a consecutive-dedup mask implementation.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Fix NumPy argmax call and implement Torch-like unique-consecutive collapsing in the ONNX greedy decoding path
steps.reason: The ONNX path is operating on NumPy arrays, so `x.argmax(dim=-1)` raises a `TypeError` (NumPy uses `axis`, not `dim`). Also, `np.unique(yseq)` removes all duplicates and sorts the result, which breaks CTC greedy collapse semantics; it must only remove adjacent duplicates while preserving order to match `torch.unique_consecutive` used in the TorchScript implementation.
steps.side_effects: Changes decoding outputs for sequences where a token repeats non-consecutively (e.g., `[1,1,2,1]` should become `[1,2,1]` rather than `[1,2]`). May affect downstream transcript comparisons/golden outputs; update or re-baseline tests if any exist. If empty sequences can occur after length slicing, guard the `np.diff` mask construction to avoid shape issues. No ONNX session/model changes expected.

step_plan.summary: Fix SenseVoice ONNXRuntime greedy CTC decoding by using `np.argmax(..., axis=-1)` and replacing `np.unique` with a consecutive-dedup mask so behavior matches the TorchScript (`torch.unique_consecutive`) path.
